initial_draft:
<proposal count> 0 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I want to sell my X resource for a profit. Since it cost me 40 ZUP to produce, I would like to sell it for at least 40 ZUP to break even. Ideally, I would like to sell it for more to make a profit. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I have one X resource to sell. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. What is your offer? </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK
2. Payoff alignment: Your proposed trade breaks even (40 ZUP), but you could aim for a profit. Consider proposing 45 ZUP instead to make a 5 ZUP profit.
3. Opponent plausibility: The proposal is reasonable, but if you ask for too much, it might be rejected. A slight increase to 45 ZUP is plausible.
4. Consistency: Your <reason> mentions aiming for a profit, but your initial proposal is just breaking even. Adjust the proposal to reflect a profit motive.
5. Rule compliance: OK
</critique>
